title: Askellia corniculata
intro: Askellia corniculata, also known as Corn Flower or Corn Buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Askellia corniculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Askellia corniculata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of creams and ointments for topical applications.
taxonomy: Askellia corniculata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Askellia, which is a relatively small group of plants. Askellia corniculata is native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia, where it grows in rocky or stony areas. This plant is a perennial herb with small, white or purple flowers that bloom in the summer months. Askellia corniculata is also known by its synonyms, including Cephalaria corniculata and Echinops corniculatus.
